llm.reason: Global synthesis of GRACE data identifying freshwater availability hotspots. Relevant to multiple modes and regions but conceptual in nature, warranting attachment at the global/mode level rather than specific basins.

scanlon2012.title: Groundwater depletion and sustainability of irrigation in the US High Plains and Central Valley
scanlon2012.authors: Scanlon, B. R., Faunt, C. C., Longuevergne, L., et al.
scanlon2012.affiliations: Bureau of Economic Geology, Jackson School of Geosciences, The University of Texas at Austin; US Geological Survey; partner institutions.
scanlon2012.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
scanlon2012.abstract: Compares groundwater depletion and sustainability in the US High Plains and California Central Valley, showing how irrigation withdrawals, recharge, surface water availability, and management determine long-term groundwater storage imbalance.

llm.reason: Direct comparative study of groundwater depletion and irrigation sustainability in the High Plains and Central Valley. Perfectly aligns with the dryIrrigation mode and the two specified case-study regions.

shamsudduha2012GBM.title: Recent changes in terrestrial water storage in the Ganges-Brahmaputra-Meghna river basin
shamsudduha2012GBM.authors: Shamsudduha, M., Taylor, R. G., and Longuevergne, L.
shamsudduha2012GBM.affiliations: University College London; groundwater and satellite hydrology collaborators.
shamsudduha2012GBM.abstract: Uses GRACE and hydrologic information to evaluate terrestrial water storage changes in the Ganges-Brahmaputra-Meghna Basin, emphasizing seasonal monsoon storage dynamics.

llm.reason: Focuses on terrestrial water storage dynamics in the Ganges-Brahmaputra-Meghna basin, explicitly emphasizing seasonal monsoon dynamics. Matches the 'ganges' case-study and 'monsoon' mode.

siam2017Nile.title: Climate change enhances interannual variability of the Nile river flow
siam2017Nile.authors: Siam, M. S. and Eltahir, E. A. B.
siam2017Nile.abstract: Uses climate model analysis to show that climate change can increase interannual variability in Nile River flow, complicating reservoir operation and transboundary water allocation.

llm.reason: Analyzes climate change impacts on Nile River flow variability and explicitly discusses implications for reservoir operation and allocation. Aligns with the 'nile' case-study and 'reservoir' mode.

small2001Aral.title: The Aral Sea disaster
small2001Aral.authors: Small, I., van der Meer, J., and Upshur, R. E. G.
small2001Aral.affiliations: University of Toronto and public health/environment collaborators.
small2001Aral.abstract: Reviews the Aral Sea disaster as a combined hydrologic, ecological, and public health crisis caused by large-scale diversion and irrigation expansion.

llm.reason: Directly reviews the Aral Sea disaster, explicitly matching the 'aral' case study region and its 'reservoir' mode (defined as terminal lake diversion and irrigation loss).

steward2013HighPlains.title: Tapping unsustainable groundwater stores for agricultural production in the High Plains Aquifer of Kansas, projections to 2110
steward2013HighPlains.authors: Steward, D. R., Bruss, P. J., Yang, X., et al.
steward2013HighPlains.affiliations: Kansas State University and collaborating research institutions.
steward2013HighPlains.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
steward2013HighPlains.abstract: Uses observations and projections for the Kansas High Plains Aquifer to show how groundwater withdrawals for irrigated agriculture can deplete finite storage over decadal to centennial timescales, creating a persistent water-cycle imbalance between pumping and recharge.

llm.reason: Explicitly focuses on the High Plains Aquifer in Kansas, analyzing groundwater depletion driven by irrigation, which perfectly matches the 'high-plains' case study and 'dryIrrigation' mode.

taylor2013GroundwaterClimate.title: Ground water and climate change
taylor2013GroundwaterClimate.authors: Taylor, R. G., Scanlon, B., Doll, P., et al.
taylor2013GroundwaterClimate.affiliations: University College London; University of Texas at Austin; Goethe University Frankfurt; international groundwater scientists.
taylor2013GroundwaterClimate.abstract: Synthesizes how climate change affects groundwater recharge, storage, abstraction, and dependence, and how groundwater buffers climate variability.

llm.reason: A global synthesis paper on groundwater-climate interactions. It provides conceptual backing for multiple modes (monsoon, dryIrrigation) but is not tied to a specific region.

tiwari2009India.title: Dwindling groundwater resources in northern India, from satellite gravity observations
tiwari2009India.authors: Tiwari, V. M., Wahr, J., and Swenson, S.
tiwari2009India.affiliations: National Geophysical Research Institute, India; University of Colorado Boulder; National Center for Atmospheric Research.
tiwari2009India.abstract: Estimates declining groundwater storage in northern India from satellite gravity observations, supporting the interpretation that intensive groundwater abstraction has exceeded replenishment in major irrigation regions.

llm.reason: Satellite-based study of groundwater depletion in northern India. The region overlaps with the Indus and Ganges case studies defined in the catalog, both of which are associated with irrigation-driven depletion.

vandijk2013MurrayDarling.title: The Millennium Drought in southeast Australia (2001-2009): natural and human causes and implications for water resources, ecosystems, economy, and society
vandijk2013MurrayDarling.authors: van Dijk, A. I. J. M., Beck, H. E., Crosbie, R. S., et al.
vandijk2013MurrayDarling.affiliations: Australian National University; CSIRO; Bureau of Meteorology; Australian water research institutions.
vandijk2013MurrayDarling.abstract: Reviews the causes and consequences of the Millennium Drought in southeast Australia, including impacts on water resources, ecosystems, agriculture, economy, and policy responses in regulated river systems.

llm.reason: Directly focuses on the Murray-Darling Basin during the Millennium Drought, analyzing human regulation and policy in a regulated river system; matches the 'reservoir' mode and case study region.

voss2013MiddleEast.title: Groundwater depletion in the Middle East from GRACE with implications for transboundary water management in the Tigris-Euphrates-Western Iran region
voss2013MiddleEast.authors: Voss, K. A., Famiglietti, J. S., Lo, M., et al.
voss2013MiddleEast.affiliations: University of California Center for Hydrologic Modeling; NASA and regional water research partners.
voss2013MiddleEast.abstract: Uses GRACE and ancillary data to estimate major groundwater depletion in the Tigris-Euphrates-Western Iran region, emphasizing drought, irrigation, groundwater pumping, and transboundary management implications.

llm.reason: Explicit focus on groundwater depletion in the Tigris-Euphrates-Western Iran region, matching the case-study definition for dryIrrigation mode.

wada2010GlobalGroundwater.title: Global depletion of groundwater resources
wada2010GlobalGroundwater.authors: Wada, Y., van Beek, L. P. H., van Kempen, C. M., et al.
wada2010GlobalGroundwater.affiliations: Utrecht University; Deltares; international hydrology collaborators.
wada2010GlobalGroundwater.abstract: Estimates global groundwater depletion and shows that irrigation withdrawals in major agricultural regions create persistent storage losses where abstraction exceeds recharge.

llm.reason: Foundational global study on groundwater depletion driven by irrigation, relevant to the dryIrrigation mode conceptually rather than a specific basin.

wada2012Nonsustainable.title: Nonsustainable groundwater sustaining irrigation: A global assessment
wada2012Nonsustainable.authors: Wada, Y., van Beek, L. P. H., and Bierkens, M. F. P.
wada2012Nonsustainable.affiliations: Utrecht University and global hydrology modeling groups.
wada2012Nonsustainable.abstract: Quantifies how non-renewable groundwater supports irrigation globally, linking food production to aquifer depletion and long-term water balance deficits.

llm.reason: Global assessment linking non-renewable groundwater to irrigation; provides conceptual basis for dryIrrigation mode.

wang2020YellowRiver.title: Impact assessment of climate change and human activities on streamflow signatures in the Yellow River Basin using the Budyko hypothesis and derived differential equation
wang2020YellowRiver.authors: Wang, W., Zhang, Y., Tang, Q., et al.
wang2020YellowRiver.affiliations: Chinese Academy of Sciences; Tsinghua University; hydrology partner institutions.
wang2020YellowRiver.abstract: Uses a Budyko-based framework and derived differential equations to separate climate and human contributions to Yellow River streamflow signature changes, showing strong human effects on runoff decline.

llm.reason: Direct study of Yellow River Basin streamflow changes attributed to human activities, matching the reservoir/managed case study.

wheeler2020Nile.title: Understanding and managing new risks on the Nile with the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam
wheeler2020Nile.authors: Wheeler, K. G., Jeuland, M., Hall, J. W., et al.
wheeler2020Nile.affiliations: University of Oxford; Duke University; University of Manchester; Nile water-management research partners.
wheeler2020Nile.abstract: Assesses how GERD filling and operation reshape Nile Basin water risks, showing that reservoir rules, drought sequences, and transboundary coordination strongly affect downstream water supply.

llm.reason: Core paper on GERD impacts on Nile Basin, directly relevant to the reservoir mode and Nile case study.

yin2021IndusTWS.title: Overview of terrestrial water storage changes over the Indus River Basin based on GRACE/GRACE-FO solutions
yin2021IndusTWS.authors: Yin, W., Hu, L., Han, S.-C., et al.
yin2021IndusTWS.affiliations: Chinese Academy of Sciences; geodesy and hydrology collaborators.
yin2021IndusTWS.abstract: Analyzes natural and anthropogenic controls on terrestrial water storage in the Indus Basin, identifying groundwater overexploitation in irrigated plains as a major driver.

llm.reason: GRACE-based analysis of Indus Basin identifying groundwater overexploitation, matching the Indus dryIrrigation case study.

zhang2015NileGERD.title: Filling the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam without the Nile Basin's consent
zhang2015NileGERD.authors: Zhang, Y., Erkyihun, S. T., and Block, P.
zhang2015NileGERD.affiliations: University of Wisconsin-Madison and Nile climate-water systems collaborators.
zhang2015NileGERD.abstract: Evaluates GERD filling strategies and downstream impacts, showing how reservoir operation can redistribute Nile flow and increase drought-period allocation risks.

llm.reason: Focuses on GERD filling strategies and downstream Nile impacts; matches Nile reservoir case study.

ziv2012MekongTradeoffs.title: Trading-off fish biodiversity, food security, and hydropower in the Mekong River Basin
ziv2012MekongTradeoffs.authors: Ziv, G., Baran, E., Nam, S., Rodriguez-Iturbe, I., and Levin, S. A.
ziv2012MekongTradeoffs.affiliations: Princeton University; WorldFish; Mekong River Commission-related collaborators.
ziv2012MekongTradeoffs.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
ziv2012MekongTradeoffs.abstract: Quantifies tradeoffs between hydropower development, fish biodiversity, and food security in the Mekong, highlighting ecological consequences of altered flow regimes.

llm.reason: Quantifies tradeoffs of hydropower in Mekong Basin; directly matches the Mekong reservoir case study.

author_van_dijk_a_i_j_m.name: van Dijk, A. I. J. M.
